projects.name: Cultural Resilience: A Post-Anthropic Theory of Bildung between Inheritances and Futurities
projects.description: Relational educational theory responding to planetary crises. Three structural moments: Rootedness (aesthetic-sensory awareness of entanglement with human and nonhuman others), Resourcefulness (diffractional world-making as creative-transformative practice), Resistance (performative destabilization of hegemonic normative orders). Draws on agential realism (Barad), relational ontology (Haraway, Latour), and critical aesthetics (Rancière, Menke).
relevance_shifts: Cultural Resilience relates Belonging (heritages, traditions, relatings, becomings), resourcefulness (Futurabilities, Reframings, Re-definitions etc.), and resistance (as as collective creative practice) and is understood as a collective human-nonhuman transformational/transgressive learning process
Articles about ecological grief, mourning, loss, Anthropocene education often operate within Rootedness without naming it
Futurability, hope, agency, planetary Bildung connect to Resourcefulness
Affective justice, relational ethics, care ethics connect to the normative dimension
The term 'resilience' itself rarely appears — the connection is structural, not lexical
Post-anthropocentric ethics, plant ethics, multispecies thinking are directly relevant
Critiques of neoliberal freedom/sovereignty connect to the freedom-as-reconfiguration thesis

projects.name: MetaKuBi — Metavorhaben zur Kulturellen Bildung in gesellschaftlichen Transformationen
projects.description: TP 1: Discourse-analytical meta-research on cultural education. TP 3: Big-data research synthesis. Systematic mapping of the field of cultural education research in the context of societal transformations (digitalization, sustainability, migration, democratic crisis).
relevance_shifts: Concepts of Transformation (e.g. Change, Morphogenesis, Metamorphosis, Transgression) and how they are contextualized
transfromative dynamics in/of/around education, arts, and aesthetic practices
Systematic reviews and meta-analyses of cultural/arts education research are directly relevant
Discourse-analytical and bibliometric approaches to mapping research fields
Institutional and organizational transformation in cultural education
Digitalization as systemic change in cultural education (not just tool adoption)
School development and Schulkultur in the context of cultural education

projects.name: AI4ArtsEd — Artificial Intelligence for Arts Education
projects.description: Participatory development of generative AI tools for arts and cultural education. Focus on bias and cultural hegemony in training data (CLIP, Stable Diffusion), critical-creative pedagogical practices (Prompt Interception), and non-extractivist approaches to AI in education. International collaboration (Kenya, Germany).
relevance_shifts: Articles about generative AI in education become lesenswert when they address cultural, aesthetic, or critical-pedagogical dimensions
AI bias, western hegemony in training data, decolonial and indigenous perspectives on AI are directly relevant
Prompt engineering as pedagogical practice, human-AI co-creation in artistic contexts
Critical AI studies that address perception, representation, or cultural production
AI literacy framed as critical practice (not just competence/skill acquisition)
Text-to-X. Image2X, constructive and deconstructive generation, CLIP, diffusion models in educational contexts
data-based empowerment, LoRa Training, Embedding Space deconstruction

projects.name: ComeArts — fortbilden durch vernetzen – vernetzen durch fortbilden
projects.description: Gelingensbedingungen diversitätssensibler, digitalisierungs- und digitalitätsbezogener Fortbildungsmodule für die Fächer Kunst und Musik in Community Networks. Professional development for arts/music teachers addressing digitalization and diversity through networked community structures. ComeArts Youth = Post-digital youth culture as a condition of teaching; ComeArts Across = Learning to explore cross-aesthetics and (post-)digitality in aesthetic education.
relevance_shifts: Post-digital teacher professional development in arts and music education
post-digital youth cultural hybrid human-nonhuman aesthetic decision making
cross-aesthetic (post-) digital practices
Diversity-sensitive approaches to arts education (intersectional, postcolonial)
Community networks and collaborative professional learning structures
Digitalization in arts/music teacher training (not generic EdTech)

projects.name: DiäS-KuBi — Digitale-Ästhetische Souveränität in der kulturellen Bildung
projects.description: Teachers' digital-aesthetic sovereignty as a basis for cultural, artistic, musical, poetic, and physical education in a digital world. Investigates how teachers can be suppoerted to develop agency at the intersection of digital and aesthetic practice. DiäS-R = Cultural Resilience. DiäS-C = Global Digital Citizenship Education
relevance_shifts: Digital-aesthetic sovereignty as concept — articles on aesthetic agency in digital contexts
Relational Teacher Training
Teacher competencies at the intersection of digital and aesthetic practice
Digitalität (not just digitalization) in arts/cultural education pedagogy
Post-digital aesthetics in educational practice
Post-digital Cultural Resilience (Belonging - Resourcefulness - Resistance)
Digital Global Citizenship Education
